source.title: Insensitivity of Place Cells to the Value of Spatial Goals in a Two-Choice Flexible Navigation Task

confidence_rationale: Provides direct dorsal CA1 evidence supporting the value-free spatial map hypothesis, consistent with ARC-007's framing. Rats showed clear goal-value behavioral sensitivity while dorsal CA1 place cells did not modulate firing by reward magnitude. This supports the hypothesis that dorsal hippocampus provides a value-neutral spatial substrate, with value encoding occurring elsewhere. Confidence is moderate because the finding is null (no modulation) in a specific subregion and task, and the dorsal/ventral distinction means it is compatible with the Jimenez/Knudsen evidence rather than contradicting it.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Dorsal CA1 place cells maintain stable spatial representations regardless of reward magnitude at goal locations. Rats showed preference for higher-value goals behaviorally, and there was out-of-field firing at goal locations, but place cell firing rate and spatial selectivity were not modulated by goal value. The authors conclude dorsal hippocampus provides a 'value-free spatial representation' that links to value via other circuits.
mapping.ree_translation: ARC-007 holds that the hippocampal module navigates residue-field terrain R(x,t) without computing new value per se. Duvelle et al. support the dorsal CA1 component of this: the spatial substrate is value-neutral in the sense that place cell geometry does not vary with reward magnitude. However, the 'out-of-field firing' at goal locations suggests some goal-relevance modulation exists. For REE, this supports the view that the navigation-geometry layer of R(x,t) is value-independent (supporting ARC-007), while a separable layer carries goal/harm valence (consistent with MECH-073). The two claims may coexist in different hippocampal layers or subregions.
mapping.mapping_caveat: This is a dorsal CA1 finding; the value-free result explicitly does not extend to ventral hippocampus (see Jimenez 2018). REE's hippocampal module does not specify a dorsal/ventral split. The task uses spatial navigation with varying reward amounts -- a clean dissociation but not one that tests harm-avoidance residue. The null result (no modulation) is intrinsically harder to interpret than a positive effect; absence of reward-magnitude coding is not the same as absence of any value representation.
mapping.source_context: Rat dorsal CA1; tetrode electrophysiology during two-choice flexible spatial navigation task; reward magnitude manipulation (1 vs. 3 food pellets)

confidence_components.notes: Good empirical paper, JNeurosci 2019. The null-modulation finding is clean and the dissociation between behavior (goal-value sensitive) and place cells (value-insensitive) is methodologically important. Mapping fidelity is moderate-good because the dorsal CA1 finding aligns with ARC-007's value-free-navigation premise. Transfer risk is lower than Jimenez/Knudsen because the spatial navigation task is structurally closer to REE's hippocampal rollout scenario.
